title: Google Just Dropped The Singularity Bomb
summary: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis suggests humanity may be in the foothills of the singularity as AI agents perform coding, research, planning, and scientific work. The video discusses whether imperfect AI has already become useful enough to speed up real workflows across multiple domains, contrasting optimistic and skeptical expert takes on AGI proximity.
writeup: This video surveys the convergent evidence that 2026 may mark the practical onset of the singularity, anchored by Demis Hassabis narrowing his AGI prediction from 2030-2035 to 2029-2030 in under a year. The through-line is the gap between benchmark limitations and real-world impact: AI agents are already compressing science, translation, enterprise workflows, and software development by an order of magnitude. The video does not argue the question is settled but documents how the pace of change has made meaningful prediction itself increasingly difficult.
key_points: Demis Hassabis, citing his own vibe-coding experience where six-month projects now take one or two hours, narrowed his AGI arrival window from 2030-2035 to 2029-2030 within a single year, a compression he attributes to recursive self-improvement visibly accelerating release cycles from 6-12 months between major releases to weeks.

Axiom Improver produced eight mathematical papers since February, five already accepted at peer-reviewed journals, including proofs that 100% of primes are partially regular and that Ramanujan's tau function misses 100% of primes under certain conditions, representing machine solutions to century-old open problems.

SAP sustainability AI agents in beta delivered greater than 50% reduction in packaging compliance review hours, scenario simulation time cut from a full day to 20 minutes, and over 20% fewer packaging compliance errors, concrete order-of-magnitude productivity shifts rather than incremental gains.

A translation industry longitudinal metric from the company Translated shows machine translation edit time falling from 3.5 seconds per word in 2014 to roughly 2 seconds by 2022, with the curve projecting human-level editing effort by end of decade, a measurable proxy for one specific domain of singularity.

ARC-AGI 3, introduced in March 2026 to test interactive experience-based reasoning (exploring, inferring goals, building a world model over time), shows humans solving 100% of environments while frontier AI systems score below 1%, the sharpest current counterevidence that AGI has arrived by any rigorous definition.

The Chan Zuckerberg Biohub released a world model of protein biology built on ESMC trained on 2.88 billion sequences plus ESM Atlas mapping 6.8 billion proteins, alongside multi-agent systems like Co-Scientist and Robin that autonomously generate hypotheses, design experiments, and refine research questions.
